FADE IN:

EXT. BEACH - SUNRISE

Waves crash against black rocks. MARA, 30s, stands barefoot at
the waterline, holding a pair of shoes.

She drops the shoes and walks into the surf.

                    MARA (V.O.)
          The last time I saw my brother,
          he was walking into the same
          ocean.

INT. DINER - DAY

Mara sits at the counter. A DINER OWNER, 60s, pours coffee.

                    DINER OWNER
          You from out of town?

                    MARA
          Just passing through.

                    DINER OWNER
          Nobody comes here just passing
          through.

Mara looks up, smiles faintly.

                    MARA
          I used to live here.

EXT. BEACH - DAY

Mara walks along the shore, shoes in hand. The tide has gone out.

                                        CUT TO:

INT. MOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

Mara sits on the edge of the bed, staring at a photograph.

FADE OUT.
